<!--
Thanks for the PR. Fill in the sections below. Sections marked OPTIONAL
can be skipped for trivial changes (doc typos, comment edits, etc.).

If this is your first PR to the project, please also read CONTRIBUTING.md.
-->

## Summary

<!-- One paragraph. What does this PR do, and why? Lead with the WHY;
     the diff shows the WHAT. -->

## Type of change

<!-- Check the boxes that apply. Multiple are fine. -->

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that resolves an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds capability)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that changes existing behavior in a way clients can observe)
- [ ] Refactor (no behavior change, internals only)
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Test-only change
- [ ] Build / CI / tooling

## Test plan

<!-- How did you verify this works? Be specific:
       - Which test files cover the change
       - What you ran manually and what you observed
       - Edge cases you considered

     "I ran the test suite" is not a test plan. -->

## Linked issues

<!-- "Fixes #123" auto-closes the issue on merge. "Refs #123" cross-links
     without closing. -->

## Checklist

- [ ] Tests added or updated and they actually exercise the change
- [ ] Documentation updated if user-facing behavior changed
- [ ] `pre-commit run --all-files` passes
- [ ] `pytest` passes (full suite, not just the changed file)
- [ ] All commits have DCO signoff (`git commit -s`) — see `CONTRIBUTING.md`
- [ ] If this touches the open-core boundary or registry data, I've read `docs/open-core-boundary.md` first

## Breaking changes (if any)

<!-- OPTIONAL. What clients need to know if this is a breaking change?
     Migration steps, deprecation path, etc. -->

## Additional context

<!-- OPTIONAL. Screenshots, perf benchmarks, links to discussion threads,
     anything else useful for the reviewer. -->
